target_context_sentence_en: The coach tried to cow the players into silence.
construct_risk: top-10 reviewed prompt; sentence revisions already applied where needed
review_notes: The context must make the rare verb interpretable, but silence may also make the target easier; keep this as an explicit guessability check.
instrument_note: Use in the smaller top-10 pilot or the full 24-item round.

target_context_sentence_en: Reporters may dog the actor for weeks.
construct_risk: top-10 reviewed prompt; sentence revisions already applied where needed
review_notes: Animal behavior may make the target guessable; keep the relatedness and guessability checks strict.
instrument_note: Use in the smaller top-10 pilot or the full 24-item round.

target_context_sentence_en: The rain will pound the roof all night.
construct_risk: top-10 reviewed prompt; sentence revisions already applied where needed
review_notes: Verify that pound as unit or currency is familiar enough without making the hitting verb familiar.
instrument_note: Use in the smaller top-10 pilot or the full 24-item round.

target_context_sentence_en: The bike can coast down the hill.
construct_risk: top-10 reviewed prompt; sentence revisions already applied where needed
review_notes: Treat this as a watch item for guessability from motion imagery.
instrument_note: Use in the smaller top-10 pilot or the full 24-item round.

target_context_sentence_en: The hard question will floor many students.
construct_risk: top-10 reviewed prompt; sentence revisions already applied where needed
review_notes: Useful for objective target check, but relatedness and guessability need strict thresholds.
instrument_note: Use in the smaller top-10 pilot or the full 24-item round.

target_context_sentence_en: His friends may needle him about the mistake.
construct_risk: top-10 reviewed prompt; sentence revisions already applied where needed
review_notes: Because the target is informal, collect a register acceptability note in pilot norming.
instrument_note: Use in the smaller top-10 pilot or the full 24-item round.

target_context_sentence_en: The school may cap the class at thirty students.
construct_risk: top-10 reviewed prompt; sentence revisions already applied where needed
review_notes: The at-quantity frame may cue the limit meaning; target preknowledge and guessability must be separated.
instrument_note: Use in the smaller top-10 pilot or the full 24-item round.

target_context_sentence_en: The horse may bolt when it hears a loud noise.
construct_risk: top-10 reviewed prompt; sentence revisions already applied where needed
review_notes: Keep a noun-route check so the hardware noun, not the athlete name, is the known route.
instrument_note: Use in the smaller top-10 pilot or the full 24-item round.

target_context_sentence_en: The thief tried to frame another man.
construct_risk: top-10 reviewed prompt; sentence revisions already applied where needed
review_notes: Legal context remains necessary, but avoid extra advanced words around the target.
instrument_note: Use in the smaller top-10 pilot or the full 24-item round.

target_context_sentence_en: I cannot stomach his rude jokes.
construct_risk: top-10 reviewed prompt; sentence revisions already applied where needed
review_notes: Cannot plus negative object may cue tolerate; use guessability ratings to decide.
instrument_note: Use in the smaller top-10 pilot or the full 24-item round.

target_context_sentence_en: The player had to duck under the rope.
construct_risk: movement route may make the target guessable from bird behavior
review_notes: Sentence vocabulary is appropriate, but relatedness and guessability must be strict.
instrument_note: Use only if expanding beyond the top-10 pack.

target_context_sentence_en: The child began to beam at her teacher.
construct_risk: brightness metaphor may raise perceived relatedness
review_notes: Sentence is simple; norm whether learners infer smiling from light or brightness.
instrument_note: Keep as a core watch item, not a preferred final item before norming.

target_context_sentence_en: A man tried to mug a woman in the park.
construct_risk: crime-domain sentence and weak noun-route familiarity
review_notes: Non-target English is easy, but mug-cup noun familiarity must be verified.
instrument_note: Include an explicit known-noun objective check.

target_context_sentence_en: They tried to doctor the report.
construct_risk: target register and learner-list visibility unresolved
review_notes: Report context is simple; external learner-list evidence is still missing.
instrument_note: Do not treat as final-safe until visibility metrics are updated.

target_context_sentence_en: The family tried to harbor the thief.
construct_risk: known noun may be weak and target is context-bound
review_notes: Theft context is understandable, but harbor as port must pass noun familiarity.
instrument_note: Prioritize known-noun accuracy and gloss clarity.

target_context_sentence_en: The student tried to skirt the question.
construct_risk: phrase-bound and moderately transparent avoid-route
review_notes: The sentence is easy, but skirt the question is phrase-like.
instrument_note: Use as diagnostic backup unless phrase-bound targets are allowed.

target_context_sentence_en: Friends may dish about the actor after class.
construct_risk: high register and phrase-bound risk
review_notes: Dish about is colloquial and not a clean primary candidate for A2-B1.
instrument_note: Keep out of final 10 unless cleaner rows fail unexpectedly.

target_context_sentence_en: The cat may spring from the box.
construct_risk: spring as jump or coil may already be known
review_notes: Sentence is simple, but target-prior-knowledge contamination is likely.
instrument_note: Ask about spring as jump/bounce or coil before use.

target_context_sentence_en: The plane began to bank to the left.
construct_risk: aviation-domain target
review_notes: Plane context is easy, but the target may test domain knowledge.
instrument_note: Use only as a domain-heavy diagnostic reserve.

target_context_sentence_en: The crew used a line to sound the water.
construct_risk: nautical and technical target
review_notes: The sentence keeps vocabulary manageable, but the construct risk remains high.
instrument_note: Use only as a domain-heavy diagnostic reserve.

target_context_sentence_en: He may sock the bag during practice.
construct_risk: informal target verb
review_notes: Sentence vocabulary is easy, but sock as hit is register-marked.
instrument_note: Use only if informal targets are deliberately retained.

target_context_sentence_en: Please season the soup with salt.
construct_risk: seasoning transfer and food-domain cue
review_notes: The sentence is very easy, but it may directly cue the food-domain target.
instrument_note: Norm target preknowledge of seasoning before considering.

target_context_sentence_en: This problem may stump many students.
construct_risk: known noun likely weak for A2-B1
review_notes: Sentence is easy, but stump as a noun may fail familiarity.
instrument_note: Use only if known-noun objective accuracy is unexpectedly high.

target_context_sentence_en: His friends may egg him on to jump.
construct_risk: particle-dependent target egg on
review_notes: The target is semantically clean but not a clean one-word verb item.
instrument_note: Use only if phrasal-verb dependence is allowed.
